items.title: MOF: Budi95 display error due to technical glitch, quota stays at 200 litres
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Finance Ministry (MOF) has clarified that the display of a 150-litre monthly Budi95 limit seen by some users during system maintenance at 11pm last night was due to a technical error. It said the technical fault has been resolved. "The Budi95 monthly quota remains at 200 litres for eligible users, including for June 2026," the MOF said in a statement today.

items.title: Curfew imposed in New Jersey city following ICE protest violence
items.summary: NEW YORK: The mayor of a New Jersey city declared a curfew Sunday around a migrant detention centre after another night of clashes between police and protesters opposed to President Donald Trump's immigration policies. Access to the Delaney Hall facility, which is operated by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), will be prohibited from 9pm to 6am until further notice, the mayor said. Mikie Sherrill, New Jersey's Democratic governor, condemned the violence.

items.title: IT consultant loses RM1.49mil in crypto investment scam
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 63-year-old information technology consultant lost RM1.49 million after falling victim to an online cryptocurrency investment scam promoted through Facebook. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the victim lodged a report with the southwest district police headquarters' commercial crime investigation department on May 30 after realising he had been duped by a fraudulent investment scheme known as "Crypto Kakaue." "The victim claimed he came across a cryptocurrency investment advertisement on Facebook in December 2023 and expressed interest in joining the scheme.

items.title: Anwar congratulates King on his birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended his congratulations to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, in conjunction with His Majesty's official birthday celebration. Anwar, on behalf of the Madani government and the people, prayed that His Majesty, the Queen, Raja Zarith Sofiah, and all members of the royal family be blessed with continued good health and remain under the protection and grace of Allah. "His Majesty will continue to reign justly and fairly, and remain at the highest position under the Federal Constitution, in line with the role of the monarchy as protector of Islam and a symbol of unity and harmony among Malaysia's multiracial society," he said.

items.title: US takes step to halt Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms outside China
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Department of Commerce on Sunday moved to close a potential loophole that may have led companies to export the world's most advanced chips — such as Nvidia's most sophisticated Blackwell processors — to subsidiaries of Chinese companies located outside China. The unexpected guidance suggests that the United States' best AI chips may have been making their way to subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places such as Malaysia despite broader US efforts to deny Chinese firms access to semiconductors needed to develop critical AI capabilities. In the unusual weekend guidance, the department's Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) said it would enforce licence requirements for advanced chips to entities headquartered in China when those entities were located outside China.

items.title: Macron: Nothing justifies Israel's escalation in Lebanon
items.summary: PARIS: French President Emmanuel Macron on Sunday said that "nothing justifies the major escalation under way in south Lebanon" where Israeli forces have launched a new offensive against Hizbollah. In a message on X after speaking with regional leaders, Macron said it was "essential" for an agreement to be reached quickly between the United States and Iran. Macron held talks with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Oman's Sultan Haitham bin Tariq, United Arab Emirates President Mohammed bin Zayed, and his Egyptian counterpart Abdel Fattah al-Sisi.

items.title: Teen found drowned at Bagan Lalang beach after going missing while swimming with friends
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 1 — A teenage boy was found drowned at Pantai Bagan Lalang in Tanjong Sepat, Sepang after he was believed to have gone missing while swimming with his friends yesterday afternoon. Selangor senior fire officer II Mohamad Issmanto Md Pono said the body of Mohd Akhil Zamani Sanusi, 17, was found about 300 metres (m) from the shore, which is 10m from the location where he was reported missing. “The victim was found at 6.50pm by the Water Rescue Team unit using the diving method (Unsurface),” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Oil prices rise more than 2pct as Israel steps up incursion into Lebanon
items.summary: US crude futures rose US$2.37, or 2.71 per cent, to US$89.73 a barrel as of 0028 GMT. Brent futures rose US$2.16, or 2.37 per cent, to US$93.28 a barrel. The stepped-up fighting, coming just after the US hosted Israeli-Lebanon peace talks in Washington on Friday, dimmed expectations that the US and Iran could soon announce an extension to their ceasefire agreement, which had driven Brent and WTI to settle up 1.80 per cent and 1.70 per cent, respectively, on Friday.

items.title: New Structure MyKad rollout this month marks new chapter in ID card evolution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: After more than two decades, Malaysia's national identity card is entering a new phase with the rollout of the New Structure MyKad this month, featuring enhanced security measures to protect personal identities and data. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail previously said the next-generation MyKad is intended to strengthen the national identity system through the adoption of improved technology and security features. National Registration Department (NRD) director-general Datuk Badrul Hisham Alias said the New Structure MyKad will feature 53 security elements, compared with 23 in the current version.

items.title: Married couple killed as Myvi skids into oncoming traffic in heavy rain during Mersing three‑vehicle crash
items.summary: Mersing district police chief Supt Abdul Razak Abdullah Sani said his team received information regarding the accident involving a Perodua Myvi, Toyota Innova, and Chery Omoda at around 5.10pm. He said the victims, Muhammad Firdaus Muhamad Bustaman, 24, and Siti Nur Aisyah Anoor Beet Babjan Kahar, 23, from Taman Kota Masai, Pasir Gudang, sustained severe injuries and were pronounced dead at the scene. “The accident is believed to have occurred when the Perodua Myvi car, carrying Muhammad Firdaus and Siti Nur Aisyah, heading towards Mersing, lost control before veering into the opposite lane and colliding with a Toyota Innova and a Chery Omoda.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway to buy US homebuilder Taylor Morrison for US$6.8bil
items.summary: NEW YORK: Berkshire Hathaway agreed on Sunday to buy Taylor Morrison Home Corp for US$6.8 billion in cash, expanding the conglomerate's housing business. Berkshire agreed to pay US$72.50 per share for Taylor Morrison, a 24 per cent premium over the Scottsdale, Arizona-based company's closing stock price of US$58.50 on Friday. Taylor Morrison posted net income of US$782.5 million on revenue of US$8.12 billion in 2025.

items.title: Prison riot in Mexico leaves seven inmates dead
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Rival gangs clashed in a prison in a northwestern Mexican state that is home to a powerful drug cartel, leaving seven inmates dead, officials said Sunday. The fighting broke out overnight Saturday into Sunday in a prison in Culiacan, the capital of Sinaloa state. The prison houses many inmates from the cartel of the same name.

items.title: Dollar steadies as markets await signals on Iran war, central banks
items.summary: US jobs data later in the week will be in focus as Federal Reserve officials signal that the US central bank may need to raise rates if the war accelerates already-high inflation. The euro fell 0.08 per cent to US$1.165. Sterling slipped 0.07 per cent to US$1.3449.

items.title: Putrajaya late‑night error corrected as Finance Ministry assures Budi95 quota remains at 200 litres
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 1 — The Finance Ministry has clarified that a temporary system error caused some Budi95 users to see their monthly quota displayed as 150 litres during scheduled maintenance at 11pm on May 31. The ministry said the error was identified and corrected, stressing that the monthly quota remains unchanged at 200 litres for eligible users, including for June 2026. “It must be emphasised that no changes have been made to the Budi95 monthly limit,” the ministry said in a statement.

items.title: Asia stocks count on AI boom to offset Gulf risks
items.summary: Indeed, the lack of news nudged Brent up 1.90 per cent to US$92.89 a barrel, while US crude added 2.40 per cent to US$89.46. South Korea rose 1.30 per cent, after surging eight per cent last week, while Taiwan climbed almost six per cent last week. COUNTDOWN TO PAYROLLS For Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures dipped 0.30 per cent, while DAX futures eased 0.20 per cent and FTSE futures lost 0.50 per cent.

items.title: Lindung 24 Jam extends round‑the‑clock protection to over nine million Malaysian workers, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — More than nine million contributors under the Employees’ Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4) are now automatically covered against non-work-related accidents under the LINDUNG 24 JAM scheme, effective today. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the scheme, introduced through amendments to the Act, expands Malaysia’s social security protection framework by providing coverage beyond workplace-related incidents, including during public holidays and rest days. “Through the LINDUNG 24 Jam scheme, Perkeso provides eight benefits to safeguard contributors’ welfare and livelihood, including Medical Benefit, Temporary Disablement Benefit, Permanent Disablement Benefit and Dependants’ Benefit.

items.title: Pas expected to discuss future with Bersatu tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Pas syura council is expected to meet tomorrow, with discussions possibly including the party's future ties with Bersatu, which is said to be facing internal turmoil. A source said the meeting is expected to be chaired by Pas spiritual leader Datuk Hashim Jasin. "Almost all syura council members have confirmed their attendance, including Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.

items.title: Brothers die after Volkswagen skids into tree
items.summary: JEMPOL: Two brothers were killed in a road accident at Km 11 of Jalan Bahau–Kemayan here at 8.40pm last night. Jempol district police chief Superintendent Norhisham Mustapar said the crash involved a Volkswagen car carrying three brothers, who were travelling from Kuala Pilah towards Temerloh, Pahang. Norhisham said the front passenger sustained injuries and was taken to Jempol Hospital for treatment.

items.title: King salutes RMAF personnel on 68th anniversary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, conveyed their best wishes to all Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) personnel and veterans in conjunction with its 68th anniversary. His Majesty, who is also the Commander-in-Chief of the armed forces, expressed his highest appreciation for the commitment of RMAF personnel in defending the sovereignty of the nation's airspace. May they be placed among the faithful and the martyrs," His Majesty said in a Facebook post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official page today.

items.title: Call to maintain Asean centrality
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has called on Southeast Asia to remain anchored in Asean centrality and avoid being drawn into choosing sides as geopolitical tensions intensify. Delivering his remarks at the fifth plenary session of the International Institute for Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue, themed "Managing Regional Tensions amid Global Competition", he said Southeast Asia's stability had long been underpinned by Asean's role in fostering dialogue, consensus and mutual respect. "Anchored firmly in Asean centrality, external powers engaging with this region must respect Asean's norms, strategic sensitivities and regional mechanisms, not bypass them," he said.

items.title: Social media age verification begins today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia today began enforcing age verification requirements for the registration and opening of social media accounts as a proactive measure to protect children under the age of 16 from cyber threats. The move is being implemented in line with the enforcement of the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC) which also began today under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). The RMC, meanwhile, requires service providers to implement comprehensive risk assessments, stricter content governance measures and a labeling mechanism for manipulated content to curb the threat of fraud and fake news.

items.title: Body in mattress: Victim believed to be suspect's acquaintance
items.summary: MELAKA: A decomposed body found wrapped in a mattress at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga here on Thursday is believed to be an acquaintance of the suspect arrested by police. State police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the finding resulted from investigations into the suspect, who had been detained within 24 hours of the body's discovery, Kosmo reported today. It was reported that a body was found in a severely decomposed state , wrapped in a mattress with both hands tied, at a shop lot in Pokok Mangga, believed to be linked to a murder case.

items.title: Over 9 million workers covered 24/7: Socso's Lindung 24 starts today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than nine million contributors under the Employees' Social Security Act are now automatically covered against non-workplace accidents under the Lindung 24 scheme, effective today. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the initiative expands Malaysia's social security safety net, extending protection beyond working hours, including during public holidays and rest days. He said the scheme, under the Social Security Organisation (Socso) would be automatically applied to all existing formal-sector employees without requiring fresh registration by employers or workers.

items.title: AI to reshape, not replace jobs, with wage pressure seen as bigger risk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to reshape employment in Malaysia over the long term rather than broadly displace jobs, with economists saying the impact is more likely to fall on tasks and wages than full occupations. As AI has rapidly integrated into the global economy, newer economic thinking suggests the impact is more nuanced than full-scale job replacement, said economist Dr Geoffrey Williams. He told the Business Times the newer framework suggests AI will alter specific tasks within jobs rather than eliminate whole occupations.

items.title: Netanyahu pledges expanded Lebanon offensive after Beaufort seizure
items.summary: QLAYAA, Lebanon: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed to push deeper into Lebanon after his military took over the medieval castle of Beaufort on Sunday, calling it a "dramatic shift" in the campaign against Hizbollah. The Iran-backed group, meanwhile, said it targeted Israeli forces near the fortress as well as army positions and infrastructure in Shlomi and Nahariya in northern Israel, while air raid sirens blared in the Acre area. Israel pursued its strikes on Lebanon, with eight people were killed in a strike on Deir Zahrani in southern Lebanon on Sunday including three women, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

items.title: US tariff cut offers Karex RM8mil boost, but losses persist: CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tariff relief from lower US import duties could provide a near-term boost to Karex Bhd, with the condom maker potentially receiving refunds of about US$2 million (RM8 million) in the fourth quarter after tariff rates were cut to 10 per cent from 19 per cent, effective March 2026. Karex posted a higher-than-expected Q3 core net loss of RM5.1 million, compared with a core net profit of RM6.0 million a year ago, as revenue declined 13.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter. For the nine-month period (9M FY26), the group recorded a core net loss of RM0.7 million, below both CIMB Securities' and consensus' FY26 forecasts of RM5.9 million and RM5.4 million, respectively.

items.title: Colombians elect president amid rising drug violence
items.summary: BOGOTÁ: Polls started to close Sunday in Colombia's presidential election, a high-stakes vote that could bring further peace talks with armed groups or a hard-right military crackdown. Pre-election polls showed left-wing senator Ivan Cepeda narrowly ahead but facing a strong challenge from hard-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella. The vote is, in part, a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro and his "total peace" policy of negotiating with guerrillas and other drug trafficking groups.

items.title: Indonesia issues early warning for coastal flooding in Bali
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's meteorological authority on Sunday issued an early warning for potential coastal flooding across several areas of Bali, the country's famed resort island, reported Xinhua. Meteorology, Climatology, and Geophysics Agency Region III head Cahyo Nugroho said the full moon phenomenon on Sunday is expected to trigger a significant rise in sea levels. Authorities warned that the higher-than-normal tides could disrupt port operations and impact coastal settlements, salt ponds, and inland fisheries.

items.title: Malaysia unlikely to lose edge despite Indonesia FTA head start [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Indonesia's move to finalise a free trade agreement (FTA) with the European Union (EU) could sharpen price competition for Malaysian palm oil exports, but analysts say Malaysia is unlikely to lose its competitive edge in the global market. Indonesia, the world's largest palm oil exporter, concluded negotiations with the EU on Sept 23 last year. Malaysia, the second-largest exporter, shipped 17.3 million tonnes of palm oil last year.

items.title: Oversupply darkens glove sector outlook [WATCH]
items.summary: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd expects the global glove industry to remain oversupplied over the next two years, with annual production capacity projected to exceed 500 billion pieces in financial years 2026 and 2027, significantly above estimated demand of around 400 billion pieces. Against this backdrop, it said Supermax Corp Bhd could remain in the red in financial year 2027 if ASPs decline from current levels. "Malaysia has since established a dominant position in the US glove market, capturing a 65 per cent to 70 per cent market share (versus China's 1.0 per cent to 2.0 per cent), while Chinese exports are subject to tariffs of 110 per cent (comprising 100 per cent Section 301 tariffs and an additional 10 per cent global tariffs)."

items.title: Beyond oil: The Petronas–Sarawak question is really about Malaysia's future federation
items.summary: The real issue is no longer petroleum alone The public debate surrounding Petronas and Sarawak has often been framed too narrowly. Headlines naturally gravitate towards the immediate dispute: petroleum rights, legal jurisdiction, Petros, the Petroleum Development Act 1974 (PDA), and the constitutional position of Sarawak within the federation. Petronas seeks legal clarity.

items.title: Pas deputy president revives call for Muafakat Nasional with new partners [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man has called for the revival of Muafakat Nasional (MN) with a fresh line-up that includes leaders from Umno, Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's "Reset" camp and other Malay-based parties. He said a new-look MN could be what was needed to "unite the ummah". Tuan Ibrahim, who is also Perikatan Nasional deputy chairman, said he believed that reviving the alliance, which he dubbed "MN 2.0", could help to realise such efforts.

items.title: 'Move on from missile deal and find replacement' - Analysts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must pivot immediately to an alternative to the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system as there is no likelihood that Norway will change its mind on the cancellation of export permits. Defence analyst Zaki Salleh drew parallels between what Malaysia is now experiencing to the United States' Pressler Amendment in 1990 which halted military aid to Pakistan. Zaki said the US embargo on Pakistan under the Pressler Amendment was a historical cautionary example of the missile impasse.

items.title: Malaysia’s under-16 social media rule starts today: What parents need to know
items.summary: Children under 16 will no longer be allowed to register social media accounts on covered platforms. Platforms must introduce age verification measures to identify underage users. Existing users may also be required to verify their age.

items.title: Malaysia’s new online safety rules kick in today: What changes for social media users?
items.summary: Malaysia’s new Risk Mitigation Code takes effect today under the Online Safety Act 2025. Social media and online platforms must assess risks, improve content moderation and provide stronger safety tools. Platforms must introduce measures enabling users to identify AI-generated and manipulated content.

items.title: 'Cannon fire part of royal parade'
items.summary: Putrajaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Aidi Sham Mohamed said the artillery fire would be ceremonial cannon salutes by the armed forces during the "Trooping the Colours" parade for His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, at Dataran Pahlawan Negara in Presint 1. Aidi Sham said two cannon shots would be fired during the full rehearsal starting about 8.30am on Wednesday. Aidi Sham said Persiaran Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah, Lebuh Perdana Timur and the surrounding routes near Complex E would be closed from 7am on the two days.

items.title: Tourism Malaysia takes Kaamatan and Gawai celebrations to major airports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Tourism Malaysia is bringing the Kaamatan and Gawai festivals to life at three main airports by welcoming domestic and international travellers as part of the Visit Malaysia Year 2026 (VM2026) campaign. The three-day celebration began yesterday at Kota Kinabalu International Airport, followed today by Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1, said Tourism Malaysia in a statement today. Kuching International Airport is scheduled for tomorrow.

items.title: Two teenage girls drown while swimming in Sandakan quarry pond
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 31 — Two teenage girls drowned while swimming in a quarry pond at Kampung Gum-Gum here on Sunday afternoon. Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said firefighters were alerted to the incident at 5.3pm and arrived at the scene at 5.59pm. "One victim was found by members of the public before the arrival of the fire and rescue team, while the second victim was located by firefighters with the assistance of the public," he said.

items.title: Tourists pour into Penang for long weekend getaway, hotel occupancy hits up to 100pc
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 31 — The long weekend holiday in conjunction with the Wesak Day celebration and the Official Birthday of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong this week saw Penang again become the preferred tourist destination, with several popular attractions across the state crowded with visitors. Over at Penang Hill, visitors could be seen queuing for the funicular railway to enjoy the cool weather and panoramic views of Penang from the hilltop. Many also took the opportunity to capture memorable family photographs during the extended holiday weekend.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues evening thunderstorm and heavy rain warning for Pahang, Johor and Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in three states until 7pm today. In a post on Facebook, MetMalaysia said that the bad weather is expected to hit Pahang, involving Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin. The same warning was also issued in Johor involving the Mersing area, while in Sabah, the area expected to be hit by the bad weather is Sandakan (Telupid and Beluran).

items.title: Abang Johari: Sarawak must develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite technologies
items.summary: KUCHING, May 31 — Sarawak is laying the groundwork to develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite engineering, data analytics and future communication technologies ahead of its planned venture into the aerospace and satellite sectors. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the effort is crucial in line with the state’s plans to explore the aerospace and satellite technology sectors, which are expected to play a strategic role in future development. “Therefore, Sarawak must begin preparing now to develop local expertise in aerospace, satellite engineering, data analytics, and future communication technologies,” he said in his message in conjunction with the 2026 Gawai Dayak celebration today.

items.title: Viral video of alleged fight near Kaamatan celebrations in Penampang prompts calls for investigation
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 31 — A video circulating on social media allegedly showing a fight involving several individuals near the KDCA grounds during the Kaamatan celebrations has sparked widespread reactions online. The clip, which has been shared online, prompted calls from netizens for authorities to investigate the alleged incident and identify those involved. However, comments posted by social media users regarding the cause of the alleged altercation, the identities of the individuals involved, or whether alcohol was a factor remain unverified.

items.title: Sarawak’s future depends on STEM, TVET and cultural resilience, says Wan Junaidi in Gawai message
items.summary: KUCHING, May 31 — Greater emphasis must be placed on Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) among the Bumiputera community in Sarawak to avoid widening economic disparities and improve their competitiveness in the future workforce, said Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Pehin Sri Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar. Although challenging, they will guarantee a brighter and more competitive future,” he said in his Gawai Dayak message. The Head of State said the Dayak community, together with other communities in Sarawak, has played a significant role in critical sectors including agriculture, plantations, the public service, private industry and professional fields.

items.title: Can strategic partnerships still be trusted? Khaled Nordin questions international agreements after Norway missile cancellation
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia has raised concerns over what it described as the growing practice of double standards in the enforcement of international law, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. Speaking at the sixth plenary session of the 23rd IISS Shangri-La Dialogue 2026 today, the minister said violations of the international law are frequently met with inconsistent and selective reactions depending on those involved. He said international law is often treated as something that exists only on paper, observed strictly by countries of the global majority, but selectively interpreted by stronger powers whenever convenient.

items.title: Producer says decision to halt ‘Konspirasi’ screenings made internally, no outside influence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Production company Elepfilm Entertainment Sdn Bhd has emphasised that the decision to halt screenings of the film “Konspirasi” in cinemas nationwide was made internally by its management without influence from external parties. No party influenced the decision,” it said. The statement was made following various speculations about the termination of the film’s screenings since May 28.

items.title: Who’s joining Bersama? Nik Nazmi reveals breakdown of party’s first 20,000 members
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Nearly half of the first 20,000 people to join Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) had never belonged to any political party before, former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said today. Former PKR members made up 29 per cent of Bersama’s first 20,000 sign-ups. Another 6.3 per cent came from Umno, followed by PAS at 5.2 per cent.

items.title: Police rule Myanmar worker’s death in Penang entertainment outlet fire as sudden death
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 31 — Police have classified the death of a Myanmar man found in a fire at an entertainment premises on Jalan Penang last night as sudden death. Northeast District deputy police chief Supt Lee Swee Sake advised the public against speculating on the incident as it could affect police investigations into the cause of death and the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department’s (JBPM) probe into the cause of the fire. He said police received information about the incident from members of the public at 8.50pm, before firefighters later found the body of the 56-year-old victim.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds forecast for parts of Selangor, Perak, Pahang, and Johor till evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — MetMalaysia has issued an urgent thunderstorm warning for parts of four states across peninsular Malaysia until 5pm today. Affected districts are Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim in Perak, Raub and Bentong in Pahang, Hulu Selangor in Selangor, and Mersing and Kota Tinggi in Johor. The warning is activated when MetMalaysia detects thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour that are expected to arrive within one hour.

items.title: Malaysia now requires identity checks for sponsored social media ads, says deputy comms minister
items.summary: MELAKA, May 31 — All advertisers using sponsored advertisements on social media platforms will be required to verify identities in a bid to curb online fraud, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. She said the measure, which also targets digital gambling activities and the misuse of prominent individuals’ identities, is among the new responsibilities under the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). She said the code will come into force today (June 1) under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) and will apply to social media platform providers classified as licensees, namely those with more than eight million users in Malaysia.

items.title: Traffic slows on highways in peninsula as holiday volume exceeds two million vehicles
items.summary: Senior General Manager of Operations at PLUS Malaysia Berhad, Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz, said traffic on the North-South Expressway exceeded two million vehicles over the past two days, Berita Harian reported this afternoon. “For the Central Region, traffic is slow after exiting the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza towards the city centre, Gua Tempurung to Gopeng and Nilai to the Seremban Rest Area heading south. Traffic is also slow from Sedenak to Kulai and Senai to Kulai.

items.title: Khaled questions Oslo’s commitment to ties with Malaysia as missile refund hangs in balance
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia is doubtful it will recover nearly RM543 million already paid to Norway for anti-ship missiles and has delivered a veiled warning that the cancellation could harm their long-standing bilateral relationship. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin raised the issue during a bilateral meeting with his Norwegian counterpart on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue here today, Bernama reported. Norway’s Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik had earlier conveyed an apology to Malaysia over the export licence cancellation.

items.title: Five men arrested over armed brawl at flat in Port Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 31 — Police have arrested five local men suspected of being involved in a brawl at Flat Seri Perantau in Port Klang Friday. Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the suspects, aged between 18 and 47, were detained after police received a report on the incident at 4pm Friday. “Acting on information, the Klang Selatan district police headquarters (IPD) arrested five local men.

items.title: MIC respects Perikatan’s revocation of entry approval, says Vigneswaran
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — MIC said it respects Perikatan Nasional’s decision to revoke its earlier approval for the party to join the opposition coalition. According to Sinar Harian , MIC president Tan Sri SA Vigneswaran said the party had no issue with any PN component, including PAS. “When the offer was made previously, PN’s leadership was led by Bersatu and at that time MIC was indeed prepared to join it.

items.title: B15 biodiesel rollout starts June 1: Why it matters for Malaysia’s energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Malaysia is set to enter a new phase in its energy transition amid a global supply crisis, with the biodiesel blend rate in Peninsular Malaysia to be gradually raised from B10 to B15 starting tomorrow, June 1. The implementation will see B15 biodiesel comprising 15 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, known as Palm Methyl Ester (PME), and 85 per cent petroleum diesel, compared with the existing B10 blend of 10 per cent PME, without requiring engine modifications for most diesel vehicles. He said that by-products from the palm oil industry, including sludge from crude palm oil production, have the potential to be used in biodiesel and aviation fuel production, creating a new value chain in the country’s commodity sector.

items.title: Sabah embarks on five-year initiative to document multi-ethnic heritage, says Hajiji
items.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the documentation effort is important to ensure that ethnic heritage and traditions are not lost, while also strengthening the Kaamatan Festival as one of the main tourism products capable of elevating Sabah’s profile on the world stage. “Kaamatan Festival is not just a cultural celebration, but an important platform to preserve ethnic traditions, foster tolerance, and strengthen political stability and state development. Sabah has more than 30 ethnic groups.

items.title: PM calls for love, kindness and moderation in Wesak Day message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on all Malaysians to reflect on the noble values that form the foundation of harmonious community life in conjunction with the Wesak Day celebration today. He said these values, particularly wisdom, moderation, compassion and kindness towards fellow human beings, are not only celebrated within religious traditions but remain highly relevant in building a society founded on mutual understanding and respect. In a nation characterised by diverse cultures, religions and backgrounds, he said the values of love, compassion and kindness highlighted during Wesak carry profound significance, especially at a time when certain groups seek to sow discord among different races and faiths.

items.title: KLIA Aerotrain returns to full 24-hour service from midnight, says transport minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 31 — The Aerotrain at KLIA Terminal 1 will resume full 24-hour operations starting 12am, June 1. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the service is now ready after months of rigorous safety upgrades and testing by Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd in coordination with the Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) and verified by independent railway assessors. The programme included detailed inspections, system testing, validation and extended trial runs under both passenger and non-passenger conditions.

items.title: Norway apologises but sticks to decision on Malaysia missile deal
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Norwegian Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik has apologised for his government’s decision to cancel the export licence for Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) to Malaysia, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He added that Sandvik also detailed the reasons behind Norway’s cancellation decision. The Norwegian government previously revoked Malaysia’s export licence for the NSM system and its launchers, citing national security concerns.

items.title: PAS info chief questions Bersatu strength, floats split for GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 31 — Widening cracks within Perikatan Nasional (PN) were exposed today after PAS publicly questioned Bersatu’s electoral readiness and floated the possibility of going it alone at the next general election. The Islamist party’s information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari also delivered a pointed critique of Bersatu’s organisational strength and electoral performance, questioning whether continued cooperation remained viable. The Pasir Mas MP said that prolonged organisational instability would not amount to genuine strengthening of a party.

items.title: Putrajaya residents told not to panic over cannon salutes for King’s parade
items.summary: Putrajaya district police chief Aidi Sham Mohamed said the sounds would be from ceremonial cannon salutes by the Malaysian Armed Forces during the Trooping of the Colours Parade for His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, at Dataran Pahlawan Negara in Presint 1, Putrajaya. He said two ceremonial cannon shots would be fired during the full rehearsal starting at 8.30am on Wednesday (June 3). “The actual ceremony on Friday (June 5) will feature 21 cannon shots, to be fired at the same time,” he said in a statement today.
